revision_history.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added
CCB 2808
All Hubs changes
New data model format and notation; add IdentifyType
Removed Query feature

values.name: None
values.summary: No presentation.

values.name: LightOutput
values.summary: Light output of a lighting product.

values.name: VisibleIndicator
values.summary: Typically a small LED.

values.name: Display
values.summary: Presentation will be visible on display screen.

values.name: Actuator
values.summary: Presentation will be conveyed by actuator functionality such as through a window blind operation or in-wall relay.

values.name: Blink
values.summary: e.g., Light is turned on/off once.

values.name: Breathe
values.summary: e.g., Light is turned on/off over 1 second and repeated 15 times.

values.name: Okay
values.summary: e.g., Colored light turns green for 1 second; non-colored light flashes twice.

values.name: ChannelChange
values.summary: e.g., Colored light turns orange for 8 seconds; non-colored light switches to the maximum brightness for 0.5s and then minimum brightness for 7.5s.

values.name: FinishEffect
values.summary: Complete the current effect sequence before terminating. e.g., if in the middle of a breathe effect (as above), first complete the current 1s breathe effect and then terminate the effect.

values.name: StopEffect
values.summary: Terminate the effect as soon as possible.

attributes.name: IdentifyTime
attributes.summary: This attribute SHALL represent the remaining length of time, in seconds, that the endpoint will continue to identify itself. If this attribute is set to a value other than 0 then the device SHALL enter its identification state, in order to indicate to an observer which of several nodes and/or endpoints it is. It is RECOMMENDED that this state consists of flashing a light with a period of 0.5 seconds. The IdentifyTime attribute SHALL be decremented every second while in this state. If this attribute reaches or is set to the value 0 then the device SHALL terminate its identification state.

attributes.name: IdentifyType
attributes.summary: This attribute SHALL indicate how the identification state is presented to the user. This attribute SHALL contain one of the values defined in IdentifyTypeEnum. The value None SHALL NOT be used if the device is capable of presenting its identification state using one of the other methods defined in IdentifyTypeEnum.

commands.name: Identify
commands.summary: This command starts or stops the receiving device identifying itself.
commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of this command, the device SHALL set the IdentifyTime attribute to the value of the IdentifyTime field. This then starts, continues, or stops the device’s identification state as detailed in IdentifyTime.

commands.name: TriggerEffect
commands.summary: This command allows the support of feedback to the user, such as a certain light effect. It is used to allow an implementation to provide visual feedback to the user under certain circumstances such as a color light turning green when it has successfully connected to a network. The use of this command and the effects themselves are entirely up to the implementer to use whenever a visual feedback is useful but it is not the same as and does not replace the identify mechanism used during commissioning.

fields.name: EffectIdentifier
fields.summary: This field SHALL indicate the identify effect to use and SHALL contain one of the non-reserved values in EffectIdentifierEnum. All values of the EffectIdentifierEnum SHALL be supported. Implementors MAY deviate from the example light effects in EffectIdentifierEnum, but they SHOULD indicate during testing how they handle each effect.

fields.name: EffectVariant
fields.summary: This field SHALL indicate which variant of the effect, indicated in the EffectIdentifier field, SHOULD be triggered. If a device does not support the given variant, it SHALL use the default variant. This field SHALL contain one of the values in EffectVariantEnum.

commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of this command, the device SHALL execute the trigger effect indicated in the EffectIdentifier and EffectVariant fields. If the EffectVariant field specifies a variant that is not supported on the device, it SHALL execute the default variant.

revision_history.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added; CCB 1745 2100
CCB 2289
New data model format and notation

features.name: GroupNames
features.summary: The ability to store a name for a group.

values.name: GroupNames
values.summary: The ability to store a name for a group.

attributes.name: NameSupport
attributes.summary: This attribute provides legacy, read-only access to whether the Group Names feature is supported. The most significant bit, bit 7 (GroupNames), SHALL be equal to bit 0 of the FeatureMap attribute (GN Feature). All other bits SHALL be 0.

commands.name: AddGroup
commands.summary: The AddGroup command allows a client to add group membership in a particular group for the server endpoint.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: This field SHALL be used to identify the group and any associated key material to which the server endpoint is to be added.

fields.name: GroupName
fields.summary: This field MAY be set to a human-readable name for the group. If the client has no name for the group, the GroupName field SHALL be set to the empty string. Support of group names is optional and is indicated by the FeatureMap and NameSupport attribute.

commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of the AddGroup command, the server SHALL perform the following procedure: 1. If the command fields are not within constraints, the status SHALL be CONSTRAINT_ERROR, and the server continues from step 6. 2. If the receiving node requires security material to support the group ID and that material does not exist for this group ID, the status SHALL be UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS and the server continues from step 6. 3. If the server endpoint is a member of the group indicated by the GroupID, the group name SHALL be updated (if supported) to GroupName, the status SHALL be SUCCESS, and the server continues from step 6. 4. If there are no available resources to add the membership for the server endpoint, the status SHALL be RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED, and the server continues from step 6. 5. The server SHALL add the server endpoint as a member of the group indicated by the GroupID, the group name SHALL be updated (if supported) to GroupName, and the status SHALL be SUCCESS. a. If the GroupID had already been added to the Group Table because of a previous AddGroup or AddGroupIfIdentifying command and a GroupName is provided and the server supports GroupName storage, then the GroupName associated with the GroupID in the Group Table SHALL be updated to reflect the new GroupName provided for the Group, such that subsequent ViewGroup commands yield the same name for all endpoints which have a group association to the given GroupID. 6. If the AddGroup command was received as a unicast, the server SHALL generate an AddGroupResponse command with the Status field set to the evaluated status. If the AddGroup command was received as a groupcast, the server SHALL NOT generate an AddGroupResponse command. See AddGroupResponse for a description of the response command.

commands.name: ViewGroup
commands.summary: The ViewGroup command allows a client to request that the server responds with a ViewGroupResponse command containing the name string for a particular group.
commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of the ViewGroup command, the server SHALL perform the following procedure: 1. If the command fields are not within constraints, the status SHALL be CONSTRAINT_ERROR and the server continues from step 4. 2. If the server endpoint is a member of the group indicated by the GroupID, the status SHALL be SUCCESS, and the server continues from step 4. 3. Else the status SHALL be NOT_FOUND. 4. If the ViewGroup command was received as a unicast, the server SHALL generate a ViewGroupResponse command for the group, and the Status field set to the evaluated status. If the ViewGroup command was received as a groupcast, the server SHALL NOT generate a ViewGroupResponse command. See ViewGroupResponse for a description of the response command.

commands.name: GetGroupMembership
commands.summary: The GetGroupMembership command allows a client to inquire about the group membership of the server endpoint, in a number of ways.
commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of the GetGroupMembership command, the server SHALL respond with group membership information using the GetGroupMembershipResponse command as follows: If the GroupList field is empty, the server SHALL respond with all group IDs indicating the groups of which the server endpoint is a member. If the GroupList field contains at least one group ID indicating a group of which the server endpoint is a member, the server SHALL respond with each group ID indicating a group of which the server endpoint is a member that matches a group in the GroupList field. If the GroupList field contains one or more group IDs but does not contain any group ID indicating a group of which the server endpoint is a member, the server SHALL only respond if the command is unicast. The response SHALL return with an empty GroupList field.

commands.name: RemoveGroup
commands.summary: The RemoveGroup command allows a client to request that the server removes the membership for the server endpoint, if any, in a particular group.
commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of the RemoveGroup command, the server SHALL perform the following procedure: 1. If the command fields are not within constraints, the status SHALL be CONSTRAINT_ERROR and the server continues from step 4. 2. If the server endpoint is a member of the group indicated by the GroupID, the server SHALL remove the server endpoint membership in the group, the status SHALL be SUCCESS, and the server continues from step 4. 3. Else the status SHALL be NOT_FOUND. 4. If the RemoveGroup command was received as a unicast, the server SHALL generate a RemoveGroupResponse command with the Status field set to the evaluated status. If the RemoveGroup command was received as a groupcast, the server SHALL NOT generate a RemoveGroupResponse command. See RemoveGroupResponse for a description of the response command. Additionally, if the Scenes Management cluster is supported on the same endpoint, scenes associated with the indicated group SHALL be removed on that endpoint.

commands.name: RemoveAllGroups
commands.summary: The RemoveAllGroups command allows a client to direct the server to remove all group associations for the server endpoint.
commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of this command, the server SHALL remove all group memberships for the server endpoint from the Group Table. If the RemoveAllGroups command was received as unicast and a response is not suppressed, the server SHALL generate a response with the Status field set to SUCCESS. Additionally, if the Scenes Management cluster is supported on the same endpoint, all scenes, except for scenes associated with group ID 0, SHALL be removed on that endpoint.

commands.name: AddGroupIfIdentifying
commands.summary: The AddGroupIfIdentifying command allows a client to add group membership in a particular group for the server endpoint, on condition that the endpoint is identifying itself. Identifying functionality is controlled using the Identify cluster, (see Identify Cluster). For correct operation of the AddGroupIfIdentifying command, any endpoint that supports the Groups server cluster SHALL also support the Identify server cluster. This command might be used to assist configuring group membership in the absence of a commissioning tool.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: This field SHALL be used to identify the group and any associated key material to which the server endpoint is to be added.

fields.name: GroupName
fields.summary: This field MAY be set to a human-readable name for the group. If the client has no name for the group, the GroupName field SHALL be set to the empty string. Support of group names is optional and is indicated by the FeatureMap and NameSupport attribute.

commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of the AddGroupIfIdentifying command, the server SHALL perform the following procedure: 1. The server verifies that it is currently identifying itself. If the server it not currently identifying itself, the status SHALL be SUCCESS, and the server continues from step 7. 2. If the command fields are not within constraints, the status SHALL be CONSTRAINT_ERROR and the server continues from step 7. 3. If the receiving node requires security material to support the group ID, and that material does not exist for this group ID, the status SHALL be UNSUPPORTED_ACCESS and the server continues from step 7. 4. If the server endpoint is a member of the group indicated by the GroupID, the status SHALL be SUCCESS and the server continues from step 7. 5. If there are no available resources to add the membership for the server endpoint, the status SHALL be RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED and the server continues from step 7. 6. The server SHALL add the server endpoint as a member of the group indicated by the GroupID, the group name SHALL be updated (if supported) to GroupName, and the status SHALL be SUCCESS. a. If the GroupID had already been added to the Group Table because of a previous AddGroup or AddGroupIfIdentifying command and a GroupName is provided and the server supports GroupName storage, then the GroupName associated with the GroupID in the Group Table SHALL be updated to reflect the new GroupName provided for the Group, such that subsequent ViewGroup commands yield the same name for all endpoints which have a group association to the given GroupID. 7. If the AddGroupIfIdentifying command was received as unicast and the evaluated status is not SUCCESS, or if the AddGroupIfIdentifying command was received as unicast and the evaluated status is SUCCESS and a response is not suppressed, the server SHALL generate a response with the Status field set to the evaluated status.

commands.name: AddGroupResponse
commands.summary: The AddGroupResponse is sent by the Groups cluster server in response to an AddGroup command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: This field is set according to the Effect on Receipt section of the AddGroup command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: This field is set to the GroupID field of the received AddGroup command.

commands.name: ViewGroupResponse
commands.summary: The ViewGroupResponse command is sent by the Groups cluster server in response to a ViewGroup command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: This field is according to the Effect on Receipt section of the ViewGroup command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: This field is set to the GroupID field of the received ViewGroup command.

fields.name: GroupName
fields.summary: If the status is SUCCESS, and group names are supported, this field is set to the group name associated with that group in the Group Table; otherwise it is set to the empty string.

commands.name: GetGroupMembershipResponse
commands.summary: The GetGroupMembershipResponse command is sent by the Groups cluster server in response to a GetGroupMembership command.

fields.name: Capacity
fields.summary: This field SHALL contain the remaining capacity of the Group Table of the node. The following values apply: • 0 - No further groups MAY be added. • 0 < Capacity < 0xFE - Capacity holds the number of groups that MAY be added. • 0xFE - At least 1 further group MAY be added (exact number is unknown). • null - It is unknown if any further groups MAY be added.

fields.name: GroupList
fields.summary: The GroupList field SHALL contain either the group IDs of all the groups in the Group Table for which the server endpoint is a member of the group (in the case where the GroupList field of the received GetGroupMembership command was empty), or the group IDs of all the groups in the Group Table for which the server endpoint is a member of the group and for which the group ID was included in the the GroupList field of the received GetGroupMembership command (in the case where the GroupList field of the received GetGroupMembership command was not empty). Zigbee: If the total number of groups will cause the maximum payload length of a frame to be exceeded, then the GroupList field SHALL contain only as many groups as will fit.

commands.name: RemoveGroupResponse
commands.summary: The RemoveGroupResponse command is generated by the server in response to the receipt of a RemoveGroup command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: This field is according to the Effect on Receipt section of the RemoveGroup command.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: This field is set to the GroupID field of the received RemoveGroup command.

revision_history.description: Addition of OffOnly feature and associated FeatureMap entry
Addition of Dead Front behavior and associated FeatureMap entry
New data model format and notation
FeatureMap global attribute support with Level Control and Lighting feature
ZLO 1.0: StartUpOnOff
Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added; CCB 1555

values.name: AcceptOnlyWhenOn
values.summary: Indicates a command is only accepted when in On state.

values.name: Off
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to FALSE

values.name: On
values.summary: Set the OnOff attribute to TRUE

values.name: Toggle
values.summary: If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to FALSE, set the OnOff attribute to TRUE. If the previous value of the OnOff attribute is equal to TRUE, set the OnOff attribute to FALSE (toggle).

values.name: DelayedAllOff
values.summary: Delayed All Off

values.name: DyingLight
values.summary: Dying Light

values.name: DelayedOffFastFade
values.summary: Fade to off in 0.8 seconds

values.name: NoFade
values.summary: No fade

values.name: DelayedOffSlowFade
values.summary: 50% dim down in 0.8 seconds then fade to off in 12 seconds

values.name: DyingLightFadeOff
values.summary: 20% dim up in 0.5s then fade to off in 1 second

attributes.name: OnOff
attributes.summary: This attribute indicates whether the device type implemented on the endpoint is turned off or turned on, in these cases the value of the OnOff attribute equals FALSE, or TRUE respectively.

attributes.name: GlobalSceneControl
attributes.summary: In order to support the use case where the user gets back the last setting of a set of devices (e.g. level settings for lights), a global scene is introduced which is stored when the devices are turned off and recalled when the devices are turned on. The global scene is defined as the scene that is stored with group identifier 0 and scene identifier 0. This attribute is defined in order to prevent a second Off command storing the all-devices-off situation as a global scene, and to prevent a second On command destroying the current settings by going back to the global scene. This attribute SHALL be set to TRUE after the reception of a command which causes the OnOff attribute to be set to TRUE, such as a standard On command, a MoveToLevel(WithOnOff) command, a RecallScene command or a OnWithRecallGlobalScene command. This attribute is set to FALSE after reception of a OffWithEffect command.

attributes.name: OnTime
attributes.summary: This attribute specifies the length of time (in 1/10ths second) that the On state SHALL be maintained before automatically transitioning to the Off state when using the OnWithTimedOff command. This attribute can be written at any time, but writing a value only has effect when in the Timed On state. See OnWithTimedOff for more details.

attributes.name: OffWaitTime
attributes.summary: This attribute specifies the length of time (in 1/10ths second) that the Off state SHALL be guarded to prevent another OnWithTimedOff command turning the server back to its On state (e.g., when leaving a room, the lights are turned off but an occupancy sensor detects the leaving person and attempts to turn the lights back on). This attribute can be written at any time, but writing a value only has an effect when in the Timed On state followed by a transition to the Delayed Off state, or in the Delayed Off state. See OnWithTimedOff for more details.

attributes.name: StartUpOnOff
attributes.summary: This attribute SHALL define the desired startup behavior of a device when it is supplied with power and this state SHALL be reflected in the OnOff attribute. If the value is null, the OnOff attribute is set to its previous value. Otherwise, the behavior is defined in the table defining StartUpOnOffEnum. This behavior does not apply to reboots associated with OTA. After an OTA restart, the OnOff attribute SHALL return to its value prior to the restart.

commands.name: Off
commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of the Off command, a server SHALL set the OnOff attribute to FALSE. Additionally, when the OnTime attribute is supported, the server SHALL set the OnTime attribute to 0.

commands.name: On
commands.effect_on_receipt: If the OffOnly feature is supported, on receipt of the On command, an UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND failure status response SHALL be sent. Otherwise, on receipt of the On command, a server SHALL set the OnOff attribute to TRUE. Additionally, when the OnTime and OffWaitTime attributes are both supported, if the value of the OnTime attribute is equal to 0, the server SHALL set the OffWaitTime attribute to 0.

commands.name: Toggle
commands.effect_on_receipt: If the OffOnly feature is supported, on receipt of the Toggle command, an UNSUPPORTED_COMMAND failure status response SHALL be sent. Otherwise, on receipt of the Toggle command, if the value of the OnOff attribute is equal to FALSE, the server SHALL set the OnOff attribute to TRUE, otherwise, the server SHALL set the OnOff attribute to FALSE. Additionally, when the OnTime and OffWaitTime attributes are both supported, if the value of the OnOff attribute is equal to FALSE and if the value of the OnTime attribute is equal to 0, the server SHALL set the OffWaitTime attribute to 0. If the value of the OnOff attribute is equal to TRUE, the server SHALL set the OnTime attribute to 0.

commands.name: OffWithEffect
commands.summary: The OffWithEffect command allows devices to be turned off using enhanced ways of fading.

fields.name: EffectIdentifier
fields.summary: This field specifies the fading effect to use when turning the device off. This field SHALL contain one of the non-reserved values listed in EffectIdentifierEnum.

fields.name: EffectVariant
fields.summary: This field is used to indicate which variant of the effect, indicated in the EffectIdentifier field, SHOULD be triggered. If the server does not support the given variant, it SHALL use the default variant. This field is dependent on the value of the EffectIdentifier field and SHALL contain one of the non-reserved values listed in either DelayedAllOffEffectVariantEnum or DyingLightEffectVariantEnum.

commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of the OffWithEffect command the server SHALL check the value of the GlobalSceneControl attribute. If the GlobalSceneControl attribute is equal to TRUE, the server SHALL store its settings in its global scene then set the GlobalSceneControl attribute to FALSE, then set the OnOff attribute to FALSE and if the OnTime attribute is supported set the OnTime attribute to 0. If the GlobalSceneControl attribute is equal to FALSE, the server SHALL only set the OnOff attribute to FALSE.

commands.name: OnWithRecallGlobalScene
commands.summary: This command allows the recall of the settings when the device was turned off.
commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of the OnWithRecallGlobalScene command, if the GlobalSceneControl attribute is equal to TRUE, the server SHALL discard the command. If the GlobalSceneControl attribute is equal to FALSE, the Scenes Management cluster server on the same endpoint SHALL recall its global scene, updating the OnOff attribute accordingly. The OnOff server SHALL then set the GlobalSceneControl attribute to TRUE.

commands.name: OnWithTimedOff
commands.summary: This command allows devices to be turned on for a specific duration with a guarded off duration so that SHOULD the device be subsequently turned off, further OnWithTimedOff commands, received during this time, are prevented from turning the devices back on. Further OnWithTimedOff commands received while the server is turned on, will update the period that the device is turned on.

fields.name: OnOffControl
fields.summary: This field contains information on how the server is to be operated.

fields.name: OnTime
fields.summary: This field is used to adjust the value of the OnTime attribute.

fields.name: OffWaitTime
fields.summary: This field is used to adjust the value of the OffWaitTime attribute.

commands.effect_on_receipt: On receipt of this command, if the AcceptOnlyWhenOn sub-field of the OnOffControl field is set to 1, and the value of the OnOff attribute is equal to FALSE, the command SHALL be discarded. If the value of the OffWaitTime attribute is greater than zero and the value of the OnOff attribute is equal to FALSE, then the server SHALL set the OffWaitTime attribute to the minimum of the OffWaitTime attribute and the value specified in the OffWaitTime field. In all other cases, the server SHALL set the OnTime attribute to the maximum of the OnTime attribute and the value specified in the OnTime field, set the OffWaitTime attribute to the value specified in the OffWaitTime field and set the OnOff attribute to TRUE.

revision_history.description: Mandatory global ClusterRevision attribute added
Added Options attribute, state change table; ZLO 1.0; Base cluster (no change) CCB 2085 1775 2281 2147
FeatureMap support with On/Off, Lighting and Frequency features
New data model format and notation
Added Q quality for CurrentLevel, CurrentFrequency and RemainingTime attributes, added clarifications for behavior related to Step value and adjusted constraint of DefaultMoveRate

commands.name: MoveToLevel
commands.summary: Move to specified level

fields.name: Level

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Transition time

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Options mask

fields.name: OptionsOverride

commands.effect_on_receipt: Create temporary Options bitmap; Set CurrentLevel to Level over TransitionTime; Update GlobalSceneControl if OnOff attribute changes

commands.name: Move
commands.summary: Continuous move

fields.name: MoveMode
fields.summary: Move direction

fields.name: Rate
fields.summary: Move rate

fields.name: OptionsMask
fields.summary: Options mask

fields.name: OptionsOverride
fields.summary: Options override

commands.effect_on_receipt: If Rate==0, return INVALID_COMMAND; Create temporary Options bitmap; Move CurrentLevel continuously in MoveMode direction at Rate; Stop when Min/MaxLevel reached

commands.name: Step

fields.name: StepSize

fields.name: OptionsOverride

commands.effect_on_receipt: If StepSize==0, return INVALID_COMMAND; Create temporary Options bitmap; Change CurrentLevel by StepSize in StepMode direction; Adjust TransitionTime proportionally if Min/MaxLevel reached

commands.name: Stop

fields.name: OptionsMask

fields.name: OptionsOverride

commands.effect_on_receipt: Create temporary Options bitmap; Stop any ongoing MoveToLevel, Move, or Step command; Set RemainingTime to 0

revision_history.description: Initial revision

attributes.name: StateValue
attributes.summary: This represents a boolean state. The semantics of this boolean state are defined by the device type using this cluster.

events.name: StateChange
events.summary: If this event is supported, it SHALL be generated when the StateValue attribute changes.

fields.name: StateValue
fields.summary: This field SHALL indicate the new value of the StateValue attribute.

values.name: CopyAllScenes
values.summary: Copy all scenes in the scene table

values.name: SceneCount
values.summary: Number of scenes currently used in the server’s Scene Table for the associated fabric.

values.name: CurrentScene
values.summary: Scene identifier of the scene last invoked on the associated fabric, 0xFF if none.

values.name: CurrentGroup
values.summary: Group identifier of the scene last invoked on the associated fabric, or 0 if not grouped.

values.name: SceneValid
values.summary: Indicates if CurrentScene and CurrentGroup fields are valid (TRUE) or not (FALSE).

values.name: RemainingCapacity
values.summary: Remaining capacity of the Scene Table on this endpoint for the accessing fabric.

values.name: AttributeID
values.summary: Identifier of the attribute. Must have 'S' quality.

values.name: ValueUnsigned8
values.summary: Unsigned 8-bit attribute value.

values.name: ValueSigned8
values.summary: Signed 8-bit attribute value.

values.name: ValueUnsigned16
values.summary: Unsigned 16-bit attribute value.

values.name: ValueSigned16
values.summary: Signed 16-bit attribute value.

values.name: ValueUnsigned32
values.summary: Unsigned 32-bit attribute value.

values.name: ValueSigned32
values.summary: Signed 32-bit attribute value.

values.name: ValueUnsigned64
values.summary: Unsigned 64-bit attribute value.

values.name: ValueSigned64
values.summary: Signed 64-bit attribute value.

values.name: ClusterID
values.summary: Identifier of the cluster whose attributes are in the AttributeValueList.

values.name: AttributeValueList
values.summary: List of attributes and their values stored as part of a scene.

values.name: SceneGroupID
values.summary: Group identifier for which this scene applies, or 0 if not associated with a group.

values.name: SceneID
values.summary: Unique identifier for this scene within its group.

values.name: SceneName
values.summary: Name of the scene (max 16 chars). Empty string if not supported.

values.name: SceneTransitionTime
values.summary: Time in milliseconds for cluster to transition to the scene's state.

values.name: ExtensionFields
values.summary: List of extension fields for Scenes-supporting clusters on the endpoint.

attributes.name: LastConfiguredBy
attributes.summary: Node ID of the node that last configured the Scene Table. Null if unknown or not configured.

attributes.name: SceneTableSize
attributes.summary: Number of entries in the Scene Table on this endpoint, total across all fabrics. Minimum size is 16.

attributes.name: FabricSceneInfo
attributes.summary: List of fabric-scoped information about scenes on this endpoint. Max entries = supported fabrics.

commands.name: AddScene
commands.summary: Adds a scene entry to the Scene Table, or replaces an existing one.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table.

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Transition time of the scene, in milliseconds.

fields.name: SceneName
fields.summary: Name of the scene.

fields.name: ExtensionFieldSetStructs
fields.summary: List of extension fields for the scene.

commands.effect_on_receipt: If GroupID non-zero, verify Group Table entry, else INVALID_COMMAND. If ExtensionFieldSetStructs invalid, INVALID_COMMAND. If scene exists, replace; else if no capacity, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED. Add scene entry, status SUCCESS. Omit unimplemented ClusterID/AttributeID. Last entry for same ClusterID/AttributeID recorded. Update SceneTransitionTime. If unicast, generate AddSceneResponse.

commands.name: AddSceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to AddScene command, indicating status of scene addition.
commands.timing: Generated in response to a received AddScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the AddScene operation.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Corresponding GroupID from the AddScene command.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Corresponding SceneID from the AddScene command.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Generated in response to a received AddScene command.

commands.name: ViewScene
commands.summary: Requests details of a specific scene from the Scene Table.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt: If GroupID non-zero, verify Group Table entry, else INVALID_COMMAND. Verify scene entry exists, else NOT_FOUND. Retrieve scene entry, status SUCCESS. If unicast, generate ViewSceneResponse with retrieved entry and status.

commands.name: ViewSceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to ViewScene command, providing scene details.
commands.timing: Generated in response to a received ViewScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the ViewScene operation.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Corresponding GroupID from the ViewScene command.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Corresponding SceneID from the ViewScene command.

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Transition time from Scene Table entry if status SUCCESS, else omitted.

fields.name: SceneName
fields.summary: Scene name from Scene Table entry if status SUCCESS, else omitted.

fields.name: ExtensionFieldSetStructs
fields.summary: Extension field sets from Scene Table entry if status SUCCESS, else omitted.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Generated in response to a received ViewScene command.

commands.name: RemoveScene
commands.summary: Removes a specific scene from the Scene Table.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt: If GroupID non-zero, verify Group Table entry, else INVALID_COMMAND. Verify scene entry exists, else NOT_FOUND. Remove scene entry, status SUCCESS. If unicast, generate RemoveSceneResponse with status.

commands.name: RemoveSceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to RemoveScene command, indicating status of scene removal.
commands.timing: Generated in response to a received RemoveScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the RemoveScene operation.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Corresponding GroupID from the RemoveScene command.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Corresponding SceneID from the RemoveScene command.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Generated in response to a received RemoveScene command.

commands.name: RemoveAllScenes
commands.summary: Removes all scenes associated with a given group from the Scene Table.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt: If GroupID non-zero, verify Group Table entry, else INVALID_COMMAND. Remove all scenes for GroupID, status SUCCESS. If unicast, generate RemoveAllScenesResponse with status.

commands.name: RemoveAllScenesResponse
commands.summary: Response to RemoveAllScenes command, indicating status of removal.
commands.timing: Generated in response to a received RemoveAllScenes command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the RemoveAllScenes operation.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Corresponding GroupID from the RemoveAllScenes command.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Generated in response to a received RemoveAllScenes command.

commands.name: StoreScene
commands.summary: Stores the current state of the endpoint as a scene.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt: If GroupID non-zero, verify Group Table entry, else INVALID_COMMAND. If scene exists, update ExtensionFieldSets with current state; else if no capacity, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED. If new scene, add with GroupID, SceneID, TransitionTime=0, SceneName='', ExtensionFieldSets from current state. Status SUCCESS. If unicast, generate StoreSceneResponse.

commands.name: StoreSceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to StoreScene command, indicating status of scene storage.
commands.timing: Generated in response to a received StoreScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the StoreScene operation.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Corresponding GroupID from the StoreScene command.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Corresponding SceneID from the StoreScene command.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Generated in response to a received StoreScene command.

commands.name: RecallScene
commands.summary: Recalls a previously stored scene, applying its state to the endpoint.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table.

fields.name: SceneID
fields.summary: Scene identifier in the Scene Table.

fields.name: TransitionTime
fields.summary: Transition time of the scene, in milliseconds.

commands.effect_on_receipt: If GroupID non-zero, verify Group Table entry, else INVALID_COMMAND. Verify scene entry exists, else NOT_FOUND. Retrieve scene entry. For each cluster, retrieve extension field sets and set attributes/state. If no extension field set, state unchanged. If extension field set omits attributes, values unchanged. Skip unknown/missing attributes. Status SUCCESS. If unicast, generate response. Transition time from command or Scene Table entry.

commands.name: GetSceneMembership
commands.summary: Requests the scene identifiers used within a specific group.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Group identifier in the Group Table.

commands.effect_on_receipt: If GroupID non-zero, verify Group Table entry, else INVALID_COMMAND. Status SUCCESS. If unicast, generate GetSceneMembershipResponse with status.

commands.name: GetSceneMembershipResponse
commands.summary: Response to GetSceneMembership command, listing scene identifiers.
commands.timing: Generated in response to a received GetSceneMembership command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the GetSceneMembership operation.

fields.name: Capacity
fields.summary: Remaining capacity of the Scene Table for the accessing fabric. 0: no more scenes. 0xFE: at least 1. null: unknown.

fields.name: GroupID
fields.summary: Corresponding GroupID from the GetSceneMembership command.

fields.name: SceneList
fields.summary: List of scene identifiers for the GroupID if status SUCCESS, else omitted.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Generated in response to a received GetSceneMembership command.

commands.name: CopyScene
commands.summary: Copies scenes from one group/scene identifier pair to another.

fields.name: Mode
fields.summary: Information on how the scene copy is to proceed.

fields.name: GroupIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Group identifier of the source scene(s).

fields.name: SceneIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Scene identifier of the source scene.

fields.name: GroupIdentifierTo
fields.summary: Group identifier of the destination scene(s).

fields.name: SceneIdentifierTo
fields.summary: Scene identifier of the destination scene.

commands.effect_on_receipt: If GroupIdentifierFrom/To non-zero, verify Group Table entries, else INVALID_COMMAND. If CopyAllScenes=0, verify source scene exists, else NOT_FOUND. If CopyAllScenes=1, copy all scenes from GroupIdentifierFrom to GroupIdentifierTo. If CopyAllScenes=0, copy specific scene. If target scene exists, replace. If no capacity, RESOURCE_EXHAUSTED. Status SUCCESS. If unicast, generate CopySceneResponse.

commands.name: CopySceneResponse
commands.summary: Response to CopyScene command, indicating status of scene copy.
commands.timing: Generated in response to a received CopyScene command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status of the CopyScene operation.

fields.name: GroupIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Corresponding GroupIdentifierFrom from the CopyScene command.

fields.name: SceneIdentifierFrom
fields.summary: Corresponding SceneIdentifierFrom from the CopyScene command.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Generated in response to a received CopyScene command.

revision_history.description: Initial revision
MfgCode field marked non-nullable, updated text, reordered sections.

values.name: MfgCode
values.summary: Manufacturer code (Vendor ID) for manufacturer-specific tags.

values.name: Value
values.summary: Semantic tag value within a standard or manufacturer-specific namespace.

values.name: Label
values.summary: Readable text describing the mode option for user display.

values.name: Mode
values.summary: Unique identifier for the mode option.

values.name: SemanticTags
values.summary: List of semantic tags that map to the mode option.

attributes.name: Description
attributes.summary: Describes the purpose of the server in readable text.

attributes.name: StandardNamespace
attributes.summary: Indicates a single standard namespace for any standard semantic tag value.

attributes.name: SupportedModes
attributes.summary: List of supported modes that may be selected for the CurrentMode attribute.

attributes.name: CurrentMode
attributes.summary: Represents the current mode of the server.

attributes.name: StartUpMode
attributes.summary: Desired startup mode for the server when supplied with power.

attributes.name: OnMode
attributes.summary: Indicates the CurrentMode value that depends on the On/Off cluster state.

commands.name: ChangeToMode
commands.summary: Changes the current mode of the server to a new specified mode.

fields.name: NewMode
fields.summary: The new mode to transition to.

commands.effect_on_receipt: If NewMode is valid: set CurrentMode := NewMode; else: respond with INVALID_COMMAND status.

revision_history.description: Initial revision
ChangeToModeResponse command: StatusText must be provided for InvalidInMode status. Require at least one standard mode tag. Define reserved ranges for base/derived clusters

values.name: MfgCode
values.summary: Manufacturer's VendorID, determines meaning of Value field.

values.name: Value
values.summary: Mode tag within a mode tag namespace (manufacturer specific or standard). Specific enum values not listed in provided text.

values.name: Label
values.summary: Readable text describing the mode option for user display.

values.name: Mode
values.summary: Identifies the mode option, unique within SupportedModes.

values.name: ModeTags
values.summary: List of tags associated with the mode option for semantic interpretation.

values.name: Success
values.summary: Switching to the mode indicated by NewMode is allowed and possible. CurrentMode is set to NewMode.

values.name: UnsupportedMode
values.summary: NewMode field value doesn't match any entries in SupportedModes attribute.

attributes.name: SupportedModes
attributes.summary: List of supported modes that may be selected for CurrentMode. Each entry has unique Mode and Label.

attributes.name: CurrentMode
attributes.summary: Indicates the current mode of the server. Value must match an entry in SupportedModes.

attributes.name: StartUpMode
attributes.summary: Desired startup mode for the server when powered up. CurrentMode is set to StartUpMode, unless OnMode overrides. No effect if null/not implemented.

attributes.name: OnMode
attributes.summary: Indicates if CurrentMode depends on the On/Off cluster state. Null means no dependency. If not null, CurrentMode changes based on OnOff attribute.

commands.name: ChangeToMode
commands.summary: Used to change the device's current mode.

fields.name: NewMode
fields.summary: The target mode to transition to.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Device responds with ChangeToModeResponse. If NewMode invalid: Status=UnsupportedMode. If NewMode valid but transition fails: Status=product-specific error/GenericFailure. If NewMode valid & succeeds: CurrentMode:=NewMode, Status=Success. If NewMode==CurrentMode: Status=Success.

commands.name: ChangeToModeResponse
commands.summary: Response to the ChangeToMode command, indicating the result of the mode change request.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Status code indicating the result of the mode change.

fields.name: StatusText
fields.summary: Human-readable string providing additional status information.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Client receives the result of a ChangeToMode command. Status field indicates success or failure, StatusText provides optional human-readable details.

values.name: Value
values.summary: Indicates the mode tag within a mode tag namespace, either manufacturer specific or standard.

values.name: MfgCode
values.summary: Indicates the manufacturer's VendorID. If present, Value field is manufacturer-specific.

values.name: Label
values.summary: Readable text describing the mode option for user display.

values.name: Mode
values.summary: Identifies the mode option.

values.name: ModeTags
values.summary: List of tags associated with the mode option, for client semantics.

values.name: Success
values.summary: Switching to the mode indicated by NewMode is allowed and possible. CurrentMode is set to NewMode.

values.name: UnsupportedMode
values.summary: NewMode field value doesn’t match any entries in SupportedModes attribute.

values.name: GenericFailure
values.summary: Generic failure, switching to the requested mode is not allowed or possible.

values.name: InvalidInMode
values.summary: Received request cannot be handled due to the current mode of the device.

values.name: Auto
values.summary: Device decides which options, features and setting values to use.

values.name: Quick
values.summary: Mode optimizes for faster completion.

values.name: Quiet
values.summary: Device is silent or barely audible while in this mode.

values.name: LowNoise
values.summary: Mode is inherently low noise or device optimizes for it.

values.name: LowEnergy
values.summary: Device optimizes for lower energy usage in this mode, also called 'Eco mode'.

values.name: Vacation
values.summary: Mode suitable for use during vacations or other extended absences.

values.name: Min
values.summary: Mode uses the lowest available setting value.

values.name: Max
values.summary: Mode uses the highest available setting value.

values.name: Night
values.summary: Mode is recommended or suitable for use during night time.

values.name: Day
values.summary: Mode is recommended or suitable for use during day time.

attributes.name: SupportedModes
attributes.summary: List of supported modes, each unique by Mode and Label fields.

attributes.name: CurrentMode
attributes.summary: Indicates the current mode of the server. Value matches a Mode field in SupportedModes.

attributes.name: StartUpMode
attributes.summary: Desired startup mode for the server when powered up. Value matches a Mode field in SupportedModes.

attributes.name: OnMode
attributes.summary: Indicates if CurrentMode depends on On/Off cluster state. Value matches a Mode field in SupportedModes.

commands.name: ChangeToMode
commands.summary: Used to change device modes.

fields.name: NewMode
fields.summary: The mode to transition to. Must match a Mode field in SupportedModes.

commands.effect_on_receipt: Respond with ChangeToModeResponse. If NewMode unsupported: Status=UnsupportedMode. If NewMode supported but transition fails: Status=error/GenericFailure. If NewMode supported & succeeds: Status=Success, CurrentMode:=NewMode. If NewMode=CurrentMode: Status=Success.

commands.name: ChangeToModeResponse
commands.summary: Sent by the device on receipt of the ChangeToMode command.

fields.name: Status
fields.summary: Indicates the result of the ChangeToMode command.

fields.name: StatusText
fields.summary: Optional human-readable string for status. Empty if UnsupportedMode. Vendor-defined error if other failure.

revision_history.description: Initial revision
Behavior clarifications for ambiguous combinations of events; Introduction of TotalNumberOfPressesCounted == 0 when MultiPressMax is exceeded; Introduction of ActionSwitch feature flag.

features.name: LatchingSwitch
features.summary: Switch is latching; maintains its position after being pressed (or turned).

features.name: MomentarySwitch
features.summary: Switch is momentary; does not maintain its position after being pressed (or turned).

features.name: MomentarySwitchRelease
features.summary: Momentary switch supports release events generation.

features.name: MomentarySwitchLongPress
features.summary: Momentary switch supports long press detection.

features.name: MomentarySwitchMultiPress
features.summary: Momentary switch supports multi-press detection (e.g., double, triple press).

features.name: ActionSwitch
features.summary: Momentary switch targeted at specific user actions with reduced event generation.

attributes.name: NumberOfPositions
attributes.summary: Indicates the maximum number of positions the switch has (minimum 2).

attributes.name: CurrentPosition
attributes.summary: Indicates the current position of the switch (0 to NumberOfPositions-1). 0 is default/idle.

attributes.name: MultiPressMax
attributes.summary: Indicates max consecutive presses detected and reported by a momentary multi-press switch.

events.name: SwitchLatched
events.summary: Generated when the latching switch is moved to a new position, possibly delayed by debouncing.

events.name: InitialPress
events.summary: Generated when the momentary switch starts to be pressed (after debouncing).

events.name: LongPress
events.summary: Generated when the momentary switch has been pressed for a manufacturer-determined 'long' time.

events.name: ShortRelease
events.summary: Generated when a momentary switch is released after a short press.

events.name: LongRelease
events.summary: Generated when a momentary switch is released after a long press.

events.name: MultiPressOngoing
events.summary: Generated during a multi-press sequence to indicate ongoing presses.

events.name: MultiPressComplete
events.summary: Generated when a multi-press sequence is completed or aborted.
